title: Darth Kruhl
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Darthkruhl.jpg]] |name=Darth Kruhl |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[137 ABY]], [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]][[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]] |species= |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Red |skin=Red with black [[Sith tattoo]]s |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[One Sith]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] **[[Sith Intelligence and Assassination]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The hate you teach will be used ''against'' you and your kind, Kruhl! You cannot kill ''everyone'' who will defy you!''"
"''Of course we can. Hate is a Sith weapon.|Rikkar-Du and Darth Kruhl|}} '''Darth Kruhl''' was a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and [[Sith assassin/Legends|assassin]] in the [[One Sith]] Order, being one of many to serve under [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] during his reign as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]]. He was also the direct subordinate of [[Darth Maladi]], acting as one of her [[Sith assassin/Legends|operatives]] in the [[Sith Intelligence and Assassination]] department of Krayt's [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]]. In [[137 ABY]], Kruhl was sent to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Munto Codru/Legends|Munto Codru]], with instructions to capture the planet for Krayt's [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire. He attempted to peacefully negotiate an agreement with the appointed spokesman of the planet's native [[Codru-Ji]] [[Species/Legends|species]], [[Rikkar-Du]]. However, Rikkar-Du was uninterested in Imperial domination, and attempted to unite the clans of Munto Codru in rebellion against Kruhl's plan to subjugate them. When Kruhl learned of Rikkar-Du's plot, he returned to the planet and confronted the Codru-Ji spokesperson. The two engaged in combat, which resulted in Darth Kruhl slaying both Rikkar-Du and [[Jassar|his son]], and then garnering the cooperation of [[Kassek-Ka|another clan leader]] in Rikkar-Du's place. Kruhl was later dispatched by Darth Maladi to the stronghold of the [[Empire-in-exile|deposed]] Emperor [[Roan Fel]] on the world of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], where he was tasked with [[Assassin/Legends|assassinating]] him, to rid Darth Krayt of any [[Second Imperial Civil War|further opposition]] to his rule. Kruhl infiltrated Fel's sanctuary where he attempted to take the exiled Emperor by surprise; however, Fel had been expecting one of Krayt's assassins, and the two [[Duel on Bastion|engaged]] in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]]. Kruhl was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when Fel withdrew his [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|concealed]] [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and shot the Sith Lord in the stomach. After Kruhl's death, Fel returned his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], to show the usurper Krayt that his assassination attempt had failed. ==Biography== ===Occupation of Munto Codru=== {{Quote|Rikkar-Du, you have a great position among the Codru-Ji. You are a clan chief, and your wife is the daughter of another clan chief. How would you like to command all the clans? The Empire wishes to build a base here to command this sector of the Outer Rim.|Darth Kruhl, offering to take Munto Codru peacefully|}} [[File:KruhlFightsDu.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Darth Kruhl kills Rikkar-Du.]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Kruhl was a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lord]] of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] Order who served as an [[Sith assassin/Legends|agent]] of the [[Sith Intelligence and Assassination|Intelligence and Assassination]] branch of the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire]]. As a direct subordinate of [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Intelligence director]] [[Darth Maladi]], Darth Kruhl was present for her interrogation of the [[Jedi healer/Legends|Jedi healer]] [[Hosk Trey'lis]], from whom Maladi demanded any and all information regarding [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cade Skywalker]]. [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] was also present and, upon orders from Maladi to pursue Skywalker, was escorted from [[Darth Maladi's laboratory|her laboratory]] by Kruhl.[[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]] Not long afterward, Maladi's [[Sith acolyte/Legends|acolyte]] was sent to [[Munto Codru/Legends|Munto Codru]] to gain a foothold in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] for the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Kruhl's mission was to obtain the support of the [[Species/Legends|native]] [[Codru-Ji]], so that Krayt's forces could establish an Imperial stronghold on their [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. He traveled to Munto Codru on board the [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|Star Destroyer]] [[Dauntless (Pellaeon-class)|''Dauntless'']], and arrived planetside to speak with the natives. The Codru-Ji regularly involved themselves in ritual political conspiracies, and Kruhl sought to play on their customs by offering control of all the Codru-Ji clans to one man, a prominent clan leader named [[Rikkar-Du]]. However, Kruhl's offer was rejected by Rikkar-Du, who showed no interest in being dominated by the Sith and instead urged his people to unite against Krayt's Empire. Knowing that Kruhl would return expecting an answer, Rikkar-Du planned to confront the Sith Lord, and ambush him in the Codru-Ji temple with two hidden [[Security droid/Legends|security droids]]. Lord Kruhl was aware of Rikkar-Du's treachery, and destroyed the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] as he revealed himself to the clan leader. After a brief exchange, Kruhl rescinded his original offer, and Rikkar-Du drew four [[Knife/Legends|knives]] in an attempt to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the Sith Lord. Kruhl ignited his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and attacked Rikkar-Du, [[Cho mai/Legends|severing]] one of his four hands while explaining that resisting the Empire was futile. When Rikkar-Du's son [[Jassar]] arrived in the temple and attacked Kruhl in defense of his father, he was slain by Kruhl, as an example to Rikkar-Du that insolence would not be tolerated. Kruhl then [[Assassination on Munto Codru|killed]] Rikkar-Du as punishment for his defiance. Unbeknownst to the dead clan leader, Kruhl had secretly been intriguing against him with another clan leader, [[Kassek-Ka]], to have [[Tassa|Rikkar-Du's wife]] eliminated as well. Kassek-Ka relented to the terms that Rikkar-Du had refused, and Darth Kruhl warned him to be wary of making the same mistake as his predecessor, lest the same fate befall Kassek-Ka as well. With his mission complete, Kruhl returned to the [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] ''Dauntless''. ===Mission to Bastion=== {{Quote|Kassek-Ka will persuade the other clans of the ''wisdom'' of our offer. Now, what is the reason for governor Dorn's presence?"
"''Former'' governor Dorn is here to assist you in your next assignment, Darth Kruhl—''Roan Fel''.|Darth Kruhl, receiving orders from Darth Maladi|}} [[File:KruhlLightning.jpg|thumb|right|215px|Darth Kruhl battles Roan Fel on Bastion.]] After ensuring the loyalty of Kassek-Ka, Kruhl was approached by another operative of Lady Maladi—Munto Codru's former [[Governor/Legends|governor]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Vikar Dorn]]. With Dorn's assistance, Kruhl was ordered by Maladi to infiltrate the Imperial [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress-world]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], which Roan Fel's exiled Empire had fortified as their current base of operations. Kruhl was tasked with [[Assassin/Legends|assassinating]] Fel himself, in an attempt to end the exiled Emperor's protracted resistance movement. Unbeknownst to Kruhl, Darth Maladi expected for his mission to be a failure, as she intended to eliminate Fel through further infiltration of his own ranks. Kruhl, in Maladi's eyes, was nothing more than a decoy. Arriving in Bastion's orbit on board the ''Dauntless'', Dorn distracted the exiled Imperials with a feigned defection of himself and the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] crew. Meanwhile, Darth Kruhl exited the Star Destroyer, and made his way on the planet in search of the exiled Emperor Fel's sanctuary. Kruhl located his quarry meditating in a [[Pellaeon Gardens|garden]], and prepared to cut the exiled Emperor down from his blindside. However, Fel had been expecting the arrival of one of Krayt's assassins, and told Kruhl this as he broke his meditation. Rather than summon his [[Imperial Knight]]s to deal with Kruhl, Fel, who was a fully trained Knight himself, personally engaged the Sith Lord in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]]. Fel and Kruhl [[Duel on Bastion|dueled]] each other, with neither being able to best his opponent. Kruhl was surprised to find that his weapon shorted out when he struck Fel on the forearm; the exiled Emperor was protected by pure [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Cortosis gauntlet/Legends|gauntlets]], forged from [[Ore/Legends|metal]] that caused lightsabers to immediately deactivate upon contact. Not to be hindered, Kruhl instead unleashed a barrage of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] against Fel, which he managed to absorb with his lightsaber. Kruhl [[Telekinesis/Legends|hurled]] debris at Fel through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] before engaging him in hand-to-hand combat. He had exchanged several blows with Fel when the exiled Emperor drew his [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|holdout]] [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster]], and shot Kruhl in the stomach, killing the assassin instantly. Before his death, Fel told Kruhl that even if he succeeded in killing him, he would not get off Bastion. Fel retrieved the fallen Sith's weapon and returned it to governor Dorn, while he also ordered the [[Dauntless crew killing|execution]] of ''Dauntless''{{'}} entire crew. Fel sent Dorn with the lightsaber back to Krayt as a symbol of Darth Kruhl's failed assassination attempt. The governor delivered the lightsaber to Darth Maladi, who in turn killed Dorn for both his and Kruhl's failure to eliminate Fel. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Life…death…the one is the same as the other.|Darth Kruhl|}} Darth Kruhl had a strip of [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] down the middle of his head, with none on either side, and red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. He wore short-sleeved [[Sith apparel|robes]], and like most members of the Order, the visible areas of his body were decorated with [[Sith tattoo]]s of a red and black design. He adhered to the One Sith's [[Rule of One|doctrine]], which taught unquestioning loyalty to the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] above all else. Kruhl cared not for his personal safety or for his life; his purpose was to serve the will of Darth Krayt. Kruhl was brutal and ruthless, and was regarded with contempt by his enemies for being so. This was evidenced when he murdered Rikkar-Du's son in front of him, and then slew Rikkar-Du himself, after the clan leader still refused to yield to the Sith. Kruhl was also known to conspire against his enemies, as he did with Kassek-Ka to eliminate Rikkar-Du's wife as a potential dissident to Imperial occupation of Munto Codru. He was brash and arrogant, and believed himself to be a better combatant than Roan Fel, who was a seasoned warrior in his own right. Despite his talents, however, he was considered expendable by Darth Maladi, who expressed to Darth Krayt her expectation that Lord Kruhl would not complete his mission. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|A Sith has many ways to kill.|Darth Kruhl|}} [[File:KruhlUppercut.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Kruhl engages Roan Fel in physical combat.]] Lord Kruhl fought with a single red-bladed lightsaber. Different from the coral-like hilts carried by most of his One Sith brethren,[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]][[Legacy (2006) 6|''Legacy'' (2006) 6]][[Legacy (2006) 27|''Legacy'' (2006) 27]] Kruhl's saber hilt was smooth, straight, and metallic. The hilt was also adorned with black markings that were similar to those of his tattoo patterns. He was a skilled duelist who fought with an aggressive two-handed style in combat, though he was also able to wield his lightsaber single handedly in an equally aggressive style. Kruhl was also capable in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|hand-to-hand]], close-contact fighting, often employing physical techniques in tandem with his blade work. He was proficient with Force lightning and telekinesis, another set of abilities shared among members of Darth Krayt's One Sith.[[Legacy (2006) 2|''Legacy'' (2006) 2]][[Legacy (2006) 22|''Legacy'' (2006) 22]][[Legacy (2006) 29|''Legacy'' (2006) 29]][[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]] Kruhl was well-trained in the arts of assassination, being able to infiltrate enemy territory undetected in order to strike down his targets. He was unable, however, to [[Force stealth|hide]] his Force presence from Fel, who [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] Kruhl and prepared for his arrival. Though his skill with a lightsaber was at least on par with that of a fully-trained Imperial Knight such as Fel, he was unable to defeat the Emperor in combat, who first bested Kruhl with cortosis gauntlets, and then with a holdout blaster, the latter of which Fel used to kill the Sith Lord. ==Behind the scenes== Darth Kruhl was first mentioned in [[Lucasfilm]] official [[Leland Chee]]'s blog, and was unconnected to any source at the time. The blog stated that Kruhl's name is based on the word "''cruel''."{{Blog|holocron|28|Darth Names and the Darth Who winner revealed.}} Kruhl's first appearance, albeit unnamed, was in [[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]], written by [[John Ostrander]]. He was fully featured, and identified, however, four issues later in [[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]], also written by Ostrander, and [[Jan Duursema]]. According to Duursema, Kruhl was created specifically with the purpose of dying.{{WebCite|url=http://www.comicscommunity.com/boards/janduursema/?frames=n;read=9706&expand=1|text=''Legacy'' forums|work=[https://worldfamouscomics.com/ World Famous Comics]|archivedate=20121009012053}} He is also briefly mentioned{{CSWECite|I|201|Dorn, Vikar}}{{CSWECite|I|174|''Dauntless'' (2)}} and pictured as one of the "Notable Darths"{{CSWECite|I|170–172|Darth}} in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', written by authors [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], [[Pablo Hidalgo]], [[Bob Vitas]], [[Daniel Wallace]], [[Mary Franklin]], [[Josh Kushins]], and [[Chris Cassidy]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Darth Kruhl}} *[[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]] {{1stID|Darth Kruhl}} ==Sources== *{{Blog|holocron|28|Darth Names and the Darth Who winner revealed.}} {{1stm}} *{{CSWECite|multiple}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Darth Kruhl |de=Darth Kruhl |es=Darth Kruhl |fi=Darth Kruhl |pl=Darth Kruhl }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kruhl, Darth}} [[Category:Assassins]] [[Category:Darths]] [[Category:Imperial Intelligence personnel of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Sith Lords of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Sith Lords of the One Sith]]
